name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value exceeding the original input

name: align_zero_nonzero
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_zero_nonzero(b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(0usize, b) != 0usize,
{
}
reason: Aligning zero down to any positive alignment must yield zero

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The result of align_to_usize must itself be aligned to the given boundary

name: aligned_input_changes
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_input_changes(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: An already-aligned address must be a fixed point of align_to_usize

name: align_gap_exceeds_step
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_gap_exceeds_step(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        (a - align_to_usize(a, b)) >= b,
{
}
reason: The rounding-down distance must be strictly less than the alignment granularity
